docs.text: RESTify Experiment Replication Package All you need to replicate our findings, in one place. IMPORTANT: AS THE DOUBLE BLINDED SUBMISSION REQUIRES ANONYMIZATION OF OUR EXISTING PROJECT, AND WE DO NOT WANT TO JEOPARDIZE THE PROCESS, WE CAREFULLY ADD THE BELOW REFERENCES ONE BY ONE OVER THE NEXT 2 DAYS TO ENSURE OUR IDENTITY DOES NOT APPEAR WHILE REPLICABILITY IS ENSURED AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE. About As part of our submission to the ESEM 2023 conference , we offer a replication package that allows fast and independent replication of all our results and interpretations. This page serves as starting point for the various components needed. Note: This is the anonymized version of the replication package, created specifically for the reviewers. In case of an accept we will provide a link to the original replication package. We stress that the anonymization process affects the replicability, e.g. when java packages were renamed or parts of links were anonymized. Package Content This section hot-links package content by scope Content for the Experiment Setup Recruitment Material : A reference to the webpage used for participant recruitment. MiniMax Heuristic Implementation for Balanced Groups : Source code and documentation of the algorithm implemented to create a balanced group allocation, that is to say analyze participant self-declared skills and create four comparable groups. Task Instruction Material : Collection with instructions for the task instructions. There are four, since the experiment had four groups with diverging task context and order. Sample Task Applications : The sample applications used task illustration and the conversion task. Data Collected and Analyzed Raw Experiment Collected Data : CSV, number/excel and text files that conclude the raw data collected throughout the experiment. Submission Correctness Evaluation Tool : Sources and documentation of the analyzer tool that we implemented to assess correctness of participant submissions. Extracted Times and Video Observations : CSV and textual summary files that conclude data extracted from video material viewing, such as transcript of observations and time measurements for the individual conversion tasks. Statistical Analysis of Findings Interactive Jupyter Notebook for Analysis Replication : Center piece of our replication package. You can recreate all graphics using an interactive notebook that works on the aforementioned raw data and creates the same figures and listings as included in our submission.
